message:
feat(web/server): tick 194 wait_for_shutdown_signal_with_cx poll-sleep cx-threaded (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Continues the ft-xbnl0.2.3 cx-threading sweep from ticks 178-193. The
web server's shutdown-signal waiter has two cancel-watcher loops (unix
and non-unix). Both previously used the ambient `runtime_compat::sleep`,
which falls back to `Cx::current()` via thread-local. When the web
server runs under a different thread-local cx than the caller's
explicit one, the 100ms poll sleep was bound to the wrong cx. Under
LabRuntime virtual time that meant cancel could land later than the
operator intended.

Both loops now call `sleep_with_cx(cx, ...)` so the poll sleep is
bound to the caller's explicit cx, matching the pattern used across
the rest of the workflows/runner and workflows/engine cx-first sweep.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/server.rs: unix + non-unix cancel
  watcher loops use sleep_with_cx with the caller's cx
- Inline comments document the fix and reference tick 194

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
